pi_items.source: Task instruction - collection name provided in task.json
Software classification standard - scikit-learn is a computer program
Official documentation - scikit-learn is a Python machine learning library
Official website verified via web search and scikit-learn documentation

Official about page (https://scikit-learn.org/stable/about.html) - community-driven project with contributors worldwide, fiscally sponsored by NumFOCUS. Original creators include Fabian Pedregosa, Gaël Varoquaux, Alexandre Gramfort, Vincent Michel (INRIA), and many others.
MNIST is a standard dataset in machine learning
Original MNIST paper by LeCun, Y., Cortes, C., and Burges, C.J.C. published in 1998 (Proceedings of the IEEE, Vol. 86, No. 11, November 1998)

pi_items.verified_value: Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences, New York University
pi_items.source: Yann LeCun is a Professor at the Courant Institute (NYU), creator of MNIST dataset. PDF reference shows 'THE MNIST DATABASE of handwritten digits Yann LeCun, Courant Institute, NYU'
Official MNIST repository hosted at Yann LeCun's website

privileged_info_summary: The task requires cataloging two real, well-known items in Zotero: (1) Scikit-learn version 1.0.2, a Python machine learning library released December 25, 2021, with official website https://scikit-learn.org, developed as a NumFOCUS-sponsored community project by contributors including Fabian Pedregosa, Gaël Varoquaux, Alexandre Gramfort, and Vincent Michel; (2) MNIST Database, a handwritten digit dataset released in 1998 by Yann LeCun (Courant Institute, NYU), Corinna Cortes, and Christopher Burges, hosted at http://yann.lecun.com/exdb/mnist/. All metadata values in the task are accurate and correspond to real attributes of these widely-used tools and datasets.
